text.title: Webinar Format Selection and Promotion Timeline
text.summary: Format types, planning decisions, multi-channel promotion timeline, and registration page elements.

rule.statement: Select webinar format based on marketing objective before planning any content or promotion.
rule.rationale: Format determines conversion style: educational webinars nurture; demo webinars close; workshops create premium offers. Mismatched format and objective produce low post-webinar conversion.

list.item: Educational: lead generation, soft CTA, nurture path
Demo: product showcase, direct CTA to trial or purchase
Q&A/AMA: community building, relationship-first
Panel/Interview: authority building through association
Workshop: deep engagement, premium offer conversion
Launch event: product launches, urgency-based close

rule.statement: Lock topic, date, platform, and whether there is a co-host before requesting any copy from an agent.
rule.rationale: Changing these after drafting requires full regeneration of all assets; sequential locking prevents rework.

list.item: Duration: 45-60 min (30 min content + 15-30 min Q&A)
Timing: Tuesday-Thursday, 11am or 2pm in audience's primary timezone
Frequency: Monthly is sustainable for solo founders
Platforms: Zoom, Demio, Livestorm (Demio/Livestorm have better registration APIs)

rule.statement: Follow the 60-minute structure: 5 min welcome, 5 min hook, 25 min content, 5 min soft pitch, 15 min Q&A, 5 min close/CTA.
rule.rationale: Deviating from this structure — especially skipping the soft pitch before Q&A — leaves conversion on the table.

rule.statement: Start promotion 3 weeks before the event; multi-channel coverage is required — email-only promotion caps registrations.
rule.rationale: Single-channel (email-only) promotion consistently underperforms multi-channel by 3-5x in registrations.

list.item: 3 weeks before: announce, start email + social promotion
2 weeks before: guest post pitch, podcast appearance pitch, partner cross-promotion
1 week before: email reminder sequence starts (3 emails)
Day before: reminder email
Day of: final reminder 1 hour before start
After: replay email to registrants who did not attend

rule.statement: Registration form must have a maximum of 2 fields (email + name); each additional field reduces conversion by 20-30%.
rule.rationale: Friction on the registration page is the second most common cause of low signups after weak promotion.

list.item: Headline: problem statement (not "Join our webinar")
Date/time with timezone converter link
Host credibility: photo + 1-2 sentence bio
3-4 specific learning outcomes as bullets
Replay promise for those who cannot attend live
Registration rate: 3% of page visits (good), 10%+ (great)
Promotion email open rate: 25% (good), 40%+ (great)
Show-up rate: 30% of registrants live (typical), 45%+ (good)
Partner referrals: 10% of registrations (good), 30%+ (great)
